# Command-Line Help for `sanctifier`

This document contains the help content for the `sanctifier` command-line program.

## `sanctifier`

Stellar Soroban Security & Formal Verification Suite

**Usage:** `sanctifier <COMMAND>`

###### **Subcommands:**

* `analyze` ‚Äî Analyze a Soroban contract for vulnerabilities
* `attest` ‚Äî Generate (or verify) a zero-knowledge attestation that a scan passed a score threshold
* `badge` ‚Äî Generate a dynamic Sanctifier status badge
* `report` ‚Äî Generate a security report
* `init` ‚Äî Initialize Sanctifier in a new project
* `callgraph` ‚Äî Generate a Graphviz DOT call graph of cross-contract calls (env.invoke_contract)
* `update` ‚Äî Check for and download the latest Sanctifier binary
* `verify` ‚Äî Verify #[sanctify::invariant] declarations across a contract or workspace
* `prove` ‚Äî Run SMT-based formal verification on Soroban token contract invariants



## `sanctifier analyze`

Analyze a Soroban contract for vulnerabilities

**Usage:** `sanctifier analyze [OPTIONS] [PATH]`

###### **Arguments:**

* `<PATH>` ‚Äî Path to the contract directory or Cargo.toml

  Default value: `.`

###### **Options:**

* `-f`, `--format <FORMAT>` ‚Äî Output format (text, json)

  Default value: `text`
* `-l`, `--limit <LIMIT>` ‚Äî Limit for ledger entry size in bytes

  Default value: `64000`
* `--vuln-db <VULN_DB>` ‚Äî Path to a custom vulnerability database JSON file
* `--webhook-url <WEBHOOK_URLS>` ‚Äî Webhook endpoint(s) to notify when scan completes (Discord/Slack/Teams/custom)



## `sanctifier attest`

Generate (or verify) a zero-knowledge attestation that a scan passed a score threshold

**Usage:** `sanctifier attest [OPTIONS] [PATH]`

###### **Arguments:**

* `<PATH>` ‚Äî Path to the contract directory or a single .rs file

  Default value: `.`

###### **Options:**

* `-t`, `--threshold <THRESHOLD>` ‚Äî Minimum security score (0-100) the scan must reach to attest

  Default value: `90`
* `-o`, `--out <OUT>` ‚Äî Write the attestation artifact here (defaults to stdout)
* `--verify <FILE>` ‚Äî Verify an existing attestation artifact instead of generating one



## `sanctifier badge`

Generate a dynamic Sanctifier status badge

**Usage:** `sanctifier badge [OPTIONS]`

###### **Options:**

* `-r`, `--report <REPORT>` ‚Äî Path to Sanctifier JSON report (from `sanctifier analyze --format json`)

  Default value: `sanctifier-report.json`
* `--svg-output <SVG_OUTPUT>` ‚Äî Where to write generated badge SVG

  Default value: `sanctifier-security.svg`
* `--markdown-output <MARKDOWN_OUTPUT>` ‚Äî Where to write generated markdown snippet
* `--badge-url <BADGE_URL>` ‚Äî Public URL for the SVG (used by markdown output). Falls back to local SVG path



## `sanctifier report`

Generate a security report

**Usage:** `sanctifier report [OPTIONS]`

###### **Options:**

* `-o`, `--output <OUTPUT>` ‚Äî Output file path



## `sanctifier init`

Initialize Sanctifier in a new project

**Usage:** `sanctifier init [OPTIONS]`

###### **Options:**

* `-f`, `--force` ‚Äî Force overwrite existing configuration file



## `sanctifier callgraph`

Generate a Graphviz DOT call graph of cross-contract calls (env.invoke_contract)

**Usage:** `sanctifier callgraph [OPTIONS] [PATH]`

###### **Arguments:**

* `<PATH>` ‚Äî Path to a contract directory, workspace directory, or a single .rs file

  Default value: `.`

###### **Options:**

* `-o`, `--output <OUTPUT>` ‚Äî Output DOT file path

  Default value: `callgraph.dot`



## `sanctifier update`

Check for and download the latest Sanctifier binary

**Usage:** `sanctifier update`



## `sanctifier verify`

Verify #[sanctify::invariant] declarations across a contract or workspace

**Usage:** `sanctifier verify [OPTIONS] [PATH]`

###### **Arguments:**

* `<PATH>` ‚Äî Path to a contract directory, workspace directory, or a single .rs file

  Default value: `.`

###### **Options:**

* `--strict` ‚Äî Exit with a non-zero status code if any invariant cannot be proven (Refuted or Unknown). Useful in CI

  Default value: `false`
* `--json` ‚Äî Emit results as JSON instead of human-readable text

  Default value: `false`
* `--quiet` ‚Äî Suppress the summary line at the end of human-readable output

  Default value: `false`



## `sanctifier prove`

Run SMT-based formal verification on Soroban token contract invariants

**Usage:** `sanctifier prove [OPTIONS] --invariant <INVARIANT>`

###### **Options:**

* `-p`, `--path <PATH>` ‚Äî Path to the contract directory or file to verify

  Default value: `.`
* `--invariant <INVARIANT>` ‚Äî Invariant to prove: balance_non_negative | supply_conserved | no_unauthorized_mint | all
* `--output-dir <OUTPUT_DIR>` ‚Äî Directory to write proof certificates (default: <path>/.sanctifier/proofs)
* `--no-save` ‚Äî Skip saving proof certificates to disk (useful for CI smoke checks)
* `--json` ‚Äî Emit results as JSON
